subject: Late-night music from Route 64 Sports Bar
body_clean:
Mayor Okonkwo,

I live across from the bars on St. Charles and I have had it with Route 64 at 540. They run live music and the patio speakers well past midnight on the weekends. Friday the 13th it was still going at 12:40, and last Saturday the 21st the bass was shaking my windows until almost 1 a.m. I am up for work at six. I have called the bar twice and nothing changes. I would like the Village to actually do something about the hours, not just tell me to call again.

subject: Late-night music from Route 64 Sports Bar
body_clean:
Diane,

Thank you for the specifics, the dates and times really help us act. I am sorry you have been losing sleep over this. I am copying Officer Banks in our Police community liaison and Sandra Pulaski in Code Enforcement so they can run a noise check on Route 64 this weekend and follow up with the owner directly. We take operating-hours conditions on these licenses seriously, and we will get you an answer rather than just sending you back to the phone. I will stay on it personally.

subject: Patrol check - Route 64, 540 St. Charles
body_clean:
Mayor,

Followed up on the Pawlak complaint. I ran a patrol check Friday and again Saturday night. Both nights I confirmed amplified sound from the patio carrying past 11 p.m. - Saturday it was still going at 12:25. I made contact with staff and issued a warning on the noise ordinance. The manager was cooperative but said the cutoff hours have never been spelled out for them. My recommendation is that Code attach a clear operating-hours condition to the license so this is enforceable going forward. Copying Sandra to pick it up.

subject: Resolution - Route 64 operating-hours condition
body_clean:
Mayor,

Closing this out. I met with the owner, Nick Brennan, at 540 St. Charles on the 3rd. Per the noise ordinance and the conditions on his liquor license, I set a written condition that outdoor sound and patio music cease at 11:00 p.m. Sunday through Thursday and midnight Friday and Saturday. Brennan agreed and signed off; he is moving the speakers off the patio rail and will pull amplified sound at the cutoff. I documented it in the file and gave a copy to PD. Considering this resolved unless complaints recur.

subject: Noise from The Hideout Lounge on weekends
body_clean:
Mayor Okonkwo,

I rent the apartment behind The Hideout Lounge at 624 St. Charles and the weekend noise has gotten out of hand this summer. It is not so much the crowd as the heavy bass from their sound system - it comes right through the wall Friday and Saturday and runs late, well after midnight last weekend. My kids share the back bedroom and cannot sleep. I do not want to shut anybody down, I just want reasonable hours after 11 or so. Is there anything the Village can do about this?

subject: Patrol check - The Hideout, 624 St. Charles
body_clean:
Mayor,

Ran a check on the Hideout complaint over the weekend. Confirmed heavy amplified bass audible from the rear of 624 St. Charles past 11 p.m. both nights. I made contact with the bartender and issued a warning under the noise ordinance. This is the same situation and the same approach we used with Route 64 last fall - patrol confirms it, we warn, then Code sets the operating-hours condition. Recommend Sandra follow up with the owner to attach the cutoff hours to the license. Copying her now.

subject: Resolution - Hideout operating-hours condition
body_clean:
Mayor,

Met with Carl Jansen at The Hideout, 624 St. Charles, on the 1st. I applied the same operating-hours remedy we used at Route 64 last year: outdoor and amplified sound to cease at 11:00 p.m. Sunday through Thursday and midnight Friday and Saturday, as a condition of the license under the noise ordinance. I cited the Route 64 case to him directly so it is clear this is the Village's standard, not a one-off. Jansen agreed and is adding a wall buffer behind the speakers. Documented and resolved.

subject: El Faro added weekend live music - noise again
body_clean:
Mayor Okonkwo,

Me again, same stretch of St. Charles. El Faro Cantina at 612 started running live bands on the weekends this month and we are right back to it - Saturday the 11th the music went past midnight and I could hear it clear as day from my place. I know you and Officer Banks sorted out Route 64 and the Hideout the same way, with set cutoff hours, and that actually held. Could you please apply the same thing to El Faro? It worked before and I would rather not have to keep writing you every couple of years.

subject: Resolution - El Faro operating-hours condition
body_clean:
Mayor,

Following the Pawlak complaint, I spoke with Marisol Vega at El Faro Cantina, 612 St. Charles. I applied the same standard remedy we used for Route 64 and The Hideout: outdoor and amplified sound to cease at 11:00 p.m. Sunday through Thursday and midnight Friday and Saturday, as a license condition under the noise ordinance. I walked her through both prior cases so she understood this is consistent across the whole strip, not aimed at her. She cooperated fully and will keep the live sets indoors after the cutoff. Resolved; copying Officer Banks.

subject: El Faro added weekend live music - noise again
body_clean:
Diane,

Good news, and thank you for flagging it early. Sandra met with the owner of El Faro this week and applied the exact same operating-hours condition we set for Route 64 and The Hideout - outdoor and amplified sound off by 11 on weeknights and midnight on Fridays and Saturdays. She cooperated and it is now in the license file. You are right that the approach has held, and we will keep treating the whole St. Charles strip the same way. Please tell me right away if the music creeps past the cutoff again. Get some sleep.
